projectDescription: A browser-based real-time radar for Counter-Strike 2 that reads game data via a C++ memory reader and broadcasts it over WebSockets, with support for local and remote sharing.
keyFeatures: Real-time player positions, health, armor, and weapons on an interactive radar map
Bomb tracking with planted timer, defuse countdown, and carrier indicator
Grenade trajectories including smoke clouds and molotov/incendiary fire zones
Kill feed and per-round scoreboard with team scores and round timer
Remote radar sharing via public IP with one-click copy link
Zoom and pan on the radar map with mouse wheel and drag support
Fullscreen mode with overlaid player panels
Fully configurable display via in-app settings (toggles and sliders)
Responsive layout supporting desktop, tablet, and mobile browsers
Exponential-backoff WebSocket reconnection with live status messages
publicApiSurface: JSON message schema: m_players, m_bomb, m_grenades, m_round_info, m_map, m_local_team
DEFAULT_SETTINGS — radar display settings object with all toggle/slider keys
